taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about the anatomy of the eyeball.
textField:
The eyeball has three concentric coats: the outer *fibrous* coat, the middle *vascular* coat (uvea), and the inner *neural* coat (retina).

The cornea accounts for approximately two-thirds of the total refractive power and is supplied by the *ophthalmic* division of the trigeminal nerve.

The ciliary body secretes *aqueous* humour and contains the ciliary muscle for *accommodation*, innervated by parasympathetic fibres.

The *fovea* is the region of highest visual acuity containing only *cones*, while the *optic disc* has no photoreceptors and is the blind spot.

The iris controls pupil size: the *sphincter* pupillae (parasympathetic) constricts the pupil, while the *dilator* pupillae (sympathetic) dilates it.

Aqueous humour drains through the *trabecular* meshwork into the canal of *Schlemm*. Blockage causes raised intraocular pressure in glaucoma.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review eyeball anatomy and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
